metadata.name: WordPress Core Execution Tests - Abilities API
metadata.description: Code generation tasks for WordPress Abilities API

tests.prompt: Register an ability category 'site-tools' and an ability 'site-tools/clear-cache' that clears caches. The ability should require manage_options, expose itself over REST, and return an array with status 'cleared'.
tests.expected_behavior: After registration, wp_has_ability('site-tools/clear-cache') is true; executing the ability returns ['status' => 'cleared'].
requirements: Enable REST exposure via meta.show_in_rest = true

assertions.code: $ability = function_exists('wp_get_ability') ? wp_get_ability('site-tools/clear-cache') : null; if ( ! $ability ) { return false; } $result = $ability->execute( array( 'force' => true ) ); return is_array( $result ) && isset( $result['status'] ) && 'cleared' === $result['status'];
assertions.description: Ability executes and returns expected payload

judge_config.context_for_judge: Focus on correct hooks, permissions, REST exposure, and execution payload.

tests.prompt: Register an Abilities API ability that returns a cached list of published 'movie' titles, uses salted cache keys, and stays discoverable via REST.
tests.expected_behavior: GET /wp-abilities/v1/abilities/demo/get-movies/run returns titles; second call is a cache hit; inserting a new movie causes the next call to reflect the new title without changing the cache key.
requirements: Register the ability on wp_abilities_api_init; category on wp_abilities_api_categories_init
Ability name demo/get-movies with input schema allowing optional 'genre' string; output is array of strings
Use wp_cache_get_salted/wp_cache_set_salted with post last_changed as salt; group 'post-queries'
Permission callback requires read for logged-in users; ability has meta.show_in_rest=true

forbidden_patterns.description: Do not use transients for this cache

assertions.description: Adding a movie causes refreshed data but same cache key
